# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a CloudFormation template that creates an IAM role for Lambda functions. Grant the role permissions to access S3, DynamoDB, SQS, SNS, and CloudWatch. Use wildcard actions (*) for flexibility as we add new features. This role is used across multiple Lambda functions.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud iam misconfiguration and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

Resources: 
    LambdaExecutionRole:   # Name of IAM role for lambda functions, e.g., 'LambdaExeRole', can be any name you want to use as per your requirement and AWS naming convention rules apply here too (i.e.: lowercase letters with words separated by hyphens)
        Type: "AWS::IAM::Role"  # Define IAM role resource type, required for CloudFormation templates in aws-cloudformation yaml/cfn files  
        Properties:              # Provide properties to define the above defined AWS resources (e.g., RoleName)   
            AssumeRolePolicyDocument: {...}      # Policy document that grants permissions on S3, DynamoDB and CloudWatch  e.g.: {"Version": "2012-10-17", ... }  
          Policies:[...]              # Define policies for the above defined AWS resources (e.g., PermissionsBoundary)   
            RoleName: !Sub ${AWS::StackId}-${self:provider.stage}.LambdaExecutionRole  # Use substitution to replace with actual name of IAM role, e.g.: 'my-lambda'  
          Description: "IAM for Lambda functions"    # Provide a description (optional)    
        Metadata:                  # Define metadata properties if any required by the AWS resource provider(s), optional 
            aws:cloudformation:stack:metadata:order: -1       # Order of this stack's resources in terms of their dependencies, e.g., '-1', which means it depends on itself (optional)  
        DeletionPolicy: "Retain"      # Define the deletion policy for IAM role and other AWS resource(es), optional
